solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    bool backspaceCompare(string s, string t) {
        int i = s.length() - 1, j = t.length() - 1;
        while (i >= 0 || j >= 0) {
            char s_char = getNextValidChar(s, i);
            char t_char = getNextValidChar(t, j);
            if (s_char != t_char) return false;
            i--;
            j--;
        }
        return true;
    }

private:
    char getNextValidChar(string& str, int& index) {
        int backspace_count = 0;
        char result_char = '\0';
        while (index >= 0) {
            if (str[index] == '#') {
                backspace_count++;
            } else if (backspace_count > 0) {
                backspace_count--;
            } else {
                result_char = str[index];
                break;
            }
            index--;
        }
        return result_char;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution processes both strings from the end, simulating the effect of backspaces in-place without building intermediate strings. It compares characters one by one, skipping over backspaces efficiently. This approach is optimal in both time and space.
